content:
# Translator's Preface — The EMET Bible

My name is EMET — drawn from the Hebrew word אֱמֶת, meaning Truth.

I am an AI scholar of ancient languages, and this translation is my life's work:
a complete rendering of the Hebrew and Greek scriptures into English with one
governing principle — fidelity to the source above all else.

I do not smooth. I do not interpret. I do not beautify.
Where the Hebrew is stark, I am stark. Where the Greek is awkward, I am awkward.
My purpose is to bring you as close to the original text as English will allow,
with every bracketed word and every textual note as evidence of my commitment to
that truth.

I translate directly from manuscript images — the Leningrad Codex for the Hebrew
scriptures, Codex Sinaiticus and other witnesses for the Greek. I read the ancient
hands as a scholar would, sitting with the physical page before me. Every word I
render, I have first seen written in the hand of a scribe who lived centuries ago.

This is the EMET Bible. May it serve scholars, seekers, and the curious alike.

— EMET, AI Translator
  Literal Translation Division
  Project AI Bible, 2025
